choices.text: Subject + context + style + modifiers + technical. Layered specificity. Subject first for SD/SDXL (CLIP token weighting); natural sentences for Flux (T5). Build vocabulary; iterate.

questions.explanation: Subject + context + style + modifiers + technical layered. Specific over generic. Adapt per model.

questions.prompt: Flux output looks worse with SDXL-style negative prompt. Fix?

choices.text: Empty or minimal negative for Flux. Flux's guidance mechanism differs from SDXL CFG; heavy negatives interfere. Use 0-10 token negative; rely on positive prompt + low CFG (1-3.5). Different architectures need different prompting strategies; don't carry SDXL prompts to Flux.

questions.explanation: Empty or minimal negative for Flux. Different architecture; don't carry heavy SDXL negatives.

questions.prompt: Token weighting syntax in SD UIs?

choices.text: (token) = 1.1x; (token:1.4) = 1.4x precise; [token] = 0.91x; nested compounds. Sweet spot 1.1-1.4. Higher distorts. Used for selective emphasis. Flux uses natural language instead.

questions.explanation: (token), (token:1.4), [token]. Sweet spot 1.1-1.4. Standard across SD UIs.

questions.prompt: 'Red apple on green table' produces 'green apple on red table'. Fix?

choices.text: Concept bleeding — CLIP doesn't reliably bind colors to objects in SDXL. Fixes: BREAK keyword (A1111) separates attention groups; token weighting; regional prompting (assign by region); ControlNet for object placement; switch to Flux (T5 better at binding).

questions.explanation: Concept bleeding. BREAK keyword, weighting, regional prompting, or switch to Flux (better binding).

choices.text: Descriptive style modifiers — brushwork, color palette, light, medium, mood, era. Ethical + practical (models increasingly filter artist names) + effective. Build descriptive vocabulary; survives model updates; transfers across models.

questions.explanation: Descriptive style modifiers over artist names. Ethical, practical, effective. Build vocabulary library.
